level.description: Level of the console messages to return. Each level includes the messages of more severe levels. Defaults to "info".

all.description: Return all console messages since the beginning of the session, not just since the last navigation. Defaults to false.

filename.description: Filename to save the console messages to. If not provided, messages are returned as text.

browser_resume.name: browser_resume
browser_resume.description: Resume script execution after it was paused. When called with step set to true, execution will pause again before the next action.

step.description: When true, execution will pause again before the next action, allowing step-by-step debugging.

location.description: Pause execution at a specific <file>:<line>, e.g. "example.spec.ts:42".

browser_highlight.name: browser_highlight
browser_highlight.description: Show a persistent highlight overlay around the element on the page.

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

style.description: Additional inline CSS applied to the highlight overlay, e.g. "outline: 2px dashed red".

browser_hide_highlight.name: browser_hide_highlight
browser_hide_highlight.description: Remove a highlight overlay previously added for the element.

element.description: Human-readable element description used when adding the highlight; must match the value passed to browser_highlight.

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

browser_annotate.name: browser_annotate
browser_annotate.description: Open the Playwright Dashboard in annotation mode for the current page and wait for the user to draw annotations. Returns the annotated screenshot, ARIA snapshot, and the list of annotations.

promptText.description: The text of the prompt in case of a prompt dialog.

browser_evaluate.name: browser_evaluate
browser_evaluate.description: Evaluate JavaScript expression on page or element

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

function.description: () => { /* code */ } or (element) => { /* code */ } when element is provided

filename.description: Filename to save the result to. If not provided, result is returned as text.

paths.description: The absolute paths to the files to upload. Can be single file or multiple files. If omitted, file chooser is cancelled.

browser_drop.name: browser_drop
browser_drop.description: Drop files or MIME-typed data onto an element, as if dragged from outside the page. At least one of "paths" or "data" must be provided.

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

paths.description: Absolute paths to files to drop onto the element.

data.description: Data to drop, as a map of MIME type to string value (e.g. {"text/plain": "hello", "text/uri-list": "https://example.com"}).

browser_find.name: browser_find
browser_find.description: Search the accessibility snapshot of the current page for text or a regular expression. Returns matching snapshot nodes with a few lines of surrounding context (like search snippets), each shown under its path from the root of the tree, which is cheaper than capturing the whole snapshot when you only need to locate an element and its ref.

text.description: Plain text to search for in the page snapshot (case-insensitive substring match). Provide either text or regex, not both.

regex.description: Regular expression to search for in the page snapshot. Matching is case-sensitive by default; wrap the pattern in slashes to add flags, e.g. "/error/i" for case-insensitive. Provide either text or regex, not both.

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

value.description: Value to fill in the field. If the field is a checkbox, the value should be `true` or `false`. If the field is a combobox, the value should be the text of the option.

browser_press_key.name: browser_press_key
browser_press_key.description: Press a key on the keyboard

key.description: Name of the key to press or a character to generate, such as `ArrowLeft` or `a`

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

text.description: Text to type into the element

submit.description: Whether to submit entered text (press Enter after)

slowly.description: Whether to type one character at a time. Useful for triggering key handlers in the page. By default entire text is filled in at once.

browser_mouse_move_xy.name: browser_mouse_move_xy
browser_mouse_move_xy.description: Move mouse to a given position

browser_mouse_click_xy.name: browser_mouse_click_xy
browser_mouse_click_xy.description: Click mouse button at a given position

button.description: Button to click, defaults to left

delay.description: Time to wait between mouse down and mouse up in milliseconds, defaults to 0

browser_mouse_drag_xy.name: browser_mouse_drag_xy
browser_mouse_drag_xy.description: Drag left mouse button to a given position

button.description: Button to press, defaults to left
Button to press, defaults to left

browser_navigate_back.name: browser_navigate_back
browser_navigate_back.description: Go back to the previous page in the history

browser_network_requests.name: browser_network_requests
browser_network_requests.description: Returns a numbered list of network requests since loading the page. Use browser_network_request with the number to get full details.

static.description: Whether to include successful static resources like images, fonts, scripts, etc. Defaults to false.

filter.description: Only return requests whose URL matches this regexp (e.g. "/api/.*user").

filename.description: Filename to save the network requests to. If not provided, requests are returned as text.

browser_network_request.name: browser_network_request
browser_network_request.description: Returns full details (headers and body) of a single network request, or a single part if `part` is set. Use the number from browser_network_requests.

index.description: 1-based index of the request, as printed by browser_network_requests.

part.description: Return only this part of the request. Omit to return full details.

filename.description: Filename to save the result to. If not provided, output is returned as text.

browser_run_code_unsafe.name: browser_run_code_unsafe
browser_run_code_unsafe.description: Run a Playwright code snippet. Unsafe: executes arbitrary JavaScript in the Playwright server process and is RCE-equivalent.

code.description: A JavaScript function containing Playwright code to execute. It will be invoked with a single argument, page, which you can use for any page interaction. For example: `async (page) => { await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Submit' }).click(); return await page.title(); }`

filename.description: Load code from the specified file. If both code and filename are provided, code will be ignored.

browser_take_screenshot.name: browser_take_screenshot
browser_take_screenshot.description: Take a screenshot of the current page. You can't perform actions based on the screenshot, use browser_snapshot for actions.

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

type.description: Image format for the screenshot. Default is png.

filename.description: File name to save the screenshot to. Defaults to `page-{timestamp}.{png|jpeg}` if not specified. Prefer relative file names to stay within the output directory.

fullPage.description: When true, takes a screenshot of the full scrollable page, instead of the currently visible viewport. Cannot be used with element screenshots.

scale.description: Image resolution scale. "css" produces a screenshot sized in CSS pixels (smaller, consistent across devices). "device" produces a high-resolution screenshot using device pixels (larger, accounts for the device pixel ratio). Default is css.

browser_snapshot.name: browser_snapshot
browser_snapshot.description: Capture accessibility snapshot of the current page, this is better than screenshot

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

filename.description: Save snapshot to markdown file instead of returning it in the response.

depth.description: Limit the depth of the snapshot tree

boxes.description: Include each element's bounding box as [box=x,y,width,height] in the snapshot. Coordinates are viewport-relative, in CSS pixels (Element.getBoundingClientRect)

browser_click.name: browser_click
browser_click.description: Perform click on a web page

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

doubleClick.description: Whether to perform a double click instead of a single click

button.description: Button to click, defaults to left

browser_drag.name: browser_drag
browser_drag.description: Perform drag and drop between two elements

startElement.description: Human-readable source element description used to obtain the permission to interact with the element

startTarget.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

endElement.description: Human-readable target element description used to obtain the permission to interact with the element

endTarget.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

browser_select_option.name: browser_select_option
browser_select_option.description: Select an option in a dropdown

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

values.description: Array of values to select in the dropdown. This can be a single value or multiple values.

browser_tabs.name: browser_tabs
browser_tabs.description: List, create, close, or select a browser tab.

index.description: Tab index, used for close/select. If omitted for close, current tab is closed.

url.description: URL to navigate to in the new tab, used for new.

browser_video_chapter.name: browser_video_chapter
browser_video_chapter.description: Add a chapter marker to the video recording. Shows a full-screen chapter card with blurred backdrop.

duration.description: Duration in milliseconds to show the chapter card

browser_video_show_actions.name: browser_video_show_actions
browser_video_show_actions.description: Annotate subsequent actions performed on the page with a callout that names the action and highlights the target element. Useful while video recording or screencasting.

duration.description: How long each action annotation stays on screen, in milliseconds. Defaults to 500.

position.description: Where to place the action title relative to the page. Defaults to top-right.

cursor.description: Cursor decoration for pointer actions. "pointer" (default) animates a mouse pointer from the previous action point to the next one; "none" disables the cursor decoration.

browser_video_hide_actions.name: browser_video_hide_actions
browser_video_hide_actions.description: Stop annotating actions performed on the page.

browser_wait_for.name: browser_wait_for
browser_wait_for.description: Wait for text to appear or disappear or a specified time to pass

time.description: The time to wait in seconds

textGone.description: The text to wait for to disappear
